



Group\_Administration - Design For Retrieval (DFR) Help



# User Group Administration


User Groups can be created and managed in the System Administration menu.


 


**Note: Making changes to the system administration requires the user to be a System Admin. This is a checkbox on the user details page, and the checkbox is also visible from the main User Administration page.**
Click here for more information regarding System Admin.


 


**Pro Tip:** **It is recommended to set up groups first, so that you don’t have to configure individual user permissions repeatedly.**  

**Setting up the groups will allow you to simply add users to the group, and they will inherit those permissions.** 
 


## **Create New User Group**


1. From the System Administration page, click on **Group Administration**  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
2. Click **Create** at the bottom of the page to create a new group  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
3. Name and describe the group you are creating  

![](assets/2025_08_21_17_53_332.png)
4. Click **Create**
5. You will be directed to the Edit Group permissions page


 


## **Edit User Group**


Note: If you have just created a user group, you will be directed to the Edit Group permissions page. If you are making changes to an existing group, click on "Edit" next to the name of the user group you would like to change.


 


### Edit Group Details


On the left side of the page, you can update the group name, description, and active status.


The active checkbox is selected by default. This can be unchecked if you want to make a group inactive.


 


### Edit Group Permissions & Assign Users


The right side of the page is where you will configure permissions and assign users to the group.


 


**To Configure Permissions:**


1. Select the catalog that the group will have access to.   

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)

  

 The catalog will be named according to the environment that it is in, so it may look slightly different but will be something like the screenshot.
2. Click **Permissions** next to the catalog name to configure specific permissions.  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
3. You will be directed to the permissions menu. Here you can **Allow** access or **Deny** access to specific modules and functions.  

  

 Best Practice Tip: Only use Allow selections when setting up the permissions for users.  

  

 Note: Permissions are denied by default, even if Deny is not selected. Permissions are only granted when **Allow** is selected. There may be specific circumstances where **Deny** needs to be selected, such as if you grant Allow access to a group, and you want to Deny for a specific person in that group. If you encounter this type of scenario, please reach out to CDS for specific instruction on configuration. Denying will override any acceptance.  

  

 \*\*User permissions override group permissions.   

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
4. To allow or deny access to all permissions in a section, you can click **Select** and then choose from the dropdown menu what you would like to do.  

![A blue and white striped paper with red lines

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)

  

![](assets/2025_08_21_17_59_383.png)
5. You can set permissions for Catalog, Classification, Attribute Groups, and Attributes.  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)

  

	1. Catalog Permissions –
		1. Allow or deny access to specific modules (SmartClass, SmartFind, etc.)
		2. Allow or deny access to certain functionalities within those modules
	2. Classification Permissions –
		1. Allow or deny access to specific categories (AKA product groups/families)
	3. Attribute Groups Permissions –
		1. Allow or deny access view and edit capabilities for attributes in specific groups  
		
		 \*Attribute groups are not used much by Lifetime as of Nov 2024, but this can be changed as needed.\*
		2. **You may want to consider creating attribute groups to manage access permissions easily. For example: You can put all Marketing attributes in a group, and then allow edit access to only the Marketing User Group.**
	4. Attributes Permissions –
		1. Allow or Deny access to view or edit individual attributes
6. Configure Catalog permissions for the group. Important permissions highlighted below:
	1. General section
		1. Module access:
			1. Access catalog status module – Access SmartAnalytics
			2. Access classification module – Access SmartClass
			3. Access export module – Ability to export in ThickClient
			4. Access import module – Ability to import in ThickClient
			5. Access SmartTrack
			6. Data developer access – ability to use Data Developer in ThickClient
		2. Approve attributes
		3. Manage attributes
		4. Manage deliverables
		5. Manage UoMs
		6. View audits – pull reports showing the change history in Convergence PIM
	2. Search section
		1. Access advanced search module – Access SmartFind
		2. Manage global saved searches
		3. View violations – ability to toggle on/off the view of data quality violations
	3. Recommendation: Allow all permissions for admin members (Darrin, Brynn, Nathan, Tamra). Allow access to all modules for any CDS employee so that we can view all in Convergence PIM to provide support and guidance.
7. Configure Classification permissions for the group.
	1. The listed permissions should be straightforward, but some additional info is below:
		1. Classify items – ability to designate the appropriate category that an item should be in. This includes ability to move from one category to another.
		2. Edit items system attributes – ability to edit the system attributes for an item (System attributes can be viewed in SmartClass)
		3. Manage allowed values – ability to create/assign the AVLs (allowed values lists) for the attributes
	2. Permissions can be set at the Root level to apply to the entire catalog, or you can click the categories in the tree to set permissions at a specific level.
		1. For example: You may want certain Content Managers to view only categories that they are responsible for.


![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)




 


8. Configure Attributes permissions for the group.
	1. Permissions:
		1. Allow or deny ability to edit an attribute value
		2. Allow or deny ability to view an attribute value
	2. You can set these permissions for all attributes by clicking **All Attributes** at the top of the list. You can also allow only certain attributes by selecting Allow only for the desired ones. You can **Deny** all attributes except the ones **Allowed**. You can **allow** all attributes except the ones **denied.**


![](assets/2025_08_21_17_59_384.png)

  

  

 


9. When you are finished making selections, click **Close** at the top right of the page.  

![](assets/2025_08_21_17_59_385.png)
10. You will be returned to the **Edit Group** page.
11. Click the folder underneath the **Users** section to select which users belong to the user group.  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
12. Select the users to include in this group. Click **Save**.  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
13. Click **Save** again on the Edit Group page to confirm the updates.  

![A screenshot of a computer

Description automatically generated](data:image/png;base64,)
14. As you create groups, you will see the group list populate. From this main screen, you can **Edit** the group’s users and/or permissions.  

  

![](assets/2025_08_21_17_59_386.png)

   

  

 You can also click **Details** for a summary.
15. If desired, click **Home** at the bottom of the page to return to the main System Administration page.
